speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Where hast thou been, sister?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Killing swine.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Sister, where thou?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: A sailor’s wife had chestnuts in her lap

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: And munched and munched and munched. “Give me,” quoth I.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: “Aroint thee, witch,” the rump-fed runnion cries.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Her husband’s to Aleppo gone, master o’ th’ Tiger;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: But in a sieve I’ll thither sail,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: And, like a rat without a tail,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: I’ll do, I’ll do, and I’ll do.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: I’ll give thee a wind.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Th’ art kind.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: And I another.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: I myself have all the other,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: And the very ports they blow;

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: All the quarters that they know

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: I’ th’ shipman’s card.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: I’ll drain him dry as hay.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Sleep shall neither night nor day

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Hang upon his penthouse lid.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: He shall live a man forbid.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Weary sev’nnights, nine times nine,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Shall he dwindle, peak, and pine.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Though his bark cannot be lost,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Yet it shall be tempest-tossed.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Look what I have.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Show me, show me.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Here I have a pilot’s thumb,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Wracked as homeward he did come.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: A drum, a drum!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Macbeth doth come.

speaker: ALL
text: The Weïrd Sisters, hand in hand,

speaker: ALL
text: Posters of the sea and land,

speaker: ALL
text: Thus do go about, about,

speaker: ALL
text: Thrice to thine and thrice to mine

speaker: ALL
text: And thrice again, to make up nine.

speaker: ALL
text: Peace, the charm’s wound up.

speaker: MACBETH
text: So foul and fair a day I have not seen.

speaker: BANQUO
text: How far is ’t called to Forres?—What are these,

speaker: BANQUO
text: So withered, and so wild in their attire,

speaker: BANQUO
text: That look not like th’ inhabitants o’ th’ Earth

speaker: BANQUO
text: And yet are on ’t?—Live you? Or are you aught

speaker: BANQUO
text: That man may question? You seem to understand me

speaker: BANQUO
text: By each at once her choppy finger laying

speaker: BANQUO
text: Upon her skinny lips. You should be women,

speaker: BANQUO
text: And yet your beards forbid me to interpret

speaker: BANQUO
text: That you are so.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Speak if you can. What are you?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: All hail, Macbeth! Hail to thee, Thane of Glamis!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: All hail, Macbeth! Hail to thee, Thane of Cawdor!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: All hail, Macbeth, that shalt be king hereafter!

speaker: BANQUO
text: Good sir, why do you start and seem to fear

speaker: BANQUO
text: Things that do sound so fair?—I’ th’ name of truth,

speaker: BANQUO
text: Are you fantastical, or that indeed

speaker: BANQUO
text: Which outwardly you show? My noble partner

speaker: BANQUO
text: You greet with present grace and great prediction

speaker: BANQUO
text: Of noble having and of royal hope,

speaker: BANQUO
text: That he seems rapt withal. To me you speak not.

speaker: BANQUO
text: If you can look into the seeds of time

speaker: BANQUO
text: And say which grain will grow and which will not,

speaker: BANQUO
text: Speak, then, to me, who neither beg nor fear

speaker: BANQUO
text: Your favors nor your hate.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Hail!

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Hail!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Hail!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Lesser than Macbeth and greater.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              WITCH
text: Not so happy, yet much happier.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: Thou shalt get kings, though thou be none.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              WITCH
text: So all hail, Macbeth and Banquo!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              WITCH
text: Banquo and Macbeth, all hail!

speaker: MACBETH
text: Stay, you imperfect speakers. Tell me more.

speaker: MACBETH
text: By Sinel’s death I know I am Thane of Glamis.

speaker: MACBETH
text: But how of Cawdor? The Thane of Cawdor lives

speaker: MACBETH
text: A prosperous gentleman, and to be king

speaker: MACBETH
text: Stands not within the prospect of belief,

speaker: MACBETH
text: No more than to be Cawdor. Say from whence

speaker: MACBETH
text: You owe this strange intelligence or why

speaker: MACBETH
text: Upon this blasted heath you stop our way

speaker: MACBETH
text: With such prophetic greeting. Speak, I charge you.

speaker: BANQUO
text: The earth hath bubbles, as the water has,

speaker: BANQUO
text: And these are of them. Whither are they vanished?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Into the air, and what seemed corporal melted,

speaker: MACBETH
text: As breath into the wind. Would they had stayed!

speaker: BANQUO
text: Were such things here as we do speak about?

speaker: BANQUO
text: Or have we eaten on the insane root

speaker: BANQUO
text: That takes the reason prisoner?

speaker: MACBETH
text: Your children shall be kings.

speaker: BANQUO
text: You shall be king.

speaker: MACBETH
text: And Thane of Cawdor too. Went it not so?

speaker: BANQUO
text: To th’ selfsame tune and words.—Who’s here?

speaker: ROSS
text: The King hath happily received, Macbeth,

speaker: ROSS
text: The news of thy success, and, when he reads

speaker: ROSS
text: Thy personal venture in the rebels’ fight,

speaker: ROSS
text: His wonders and his praises do contend

speaker: ROSS
text: Which should be thine or his. Silenced with that,

speaker: ROSS
text: In viewing o’er the rest o’ th’ selfsame day

speaker: ROSS
text: He finds thee in the stout Norweyan ranks,

speaker: ROSS
text: Nothing afeard of what thyself didst make,

speaker: ROSS
text: Strange images of death. As thick as tale

speaker: ROSS
text: Came post with post, and every one did bear

speaker: ROSS
text: Thy praises in his kingdom’s great defense,

speaker: ROSS
text: And poured them down before him.

speaker: ANGUS
text: We are sent

speaker: ANGUS
text: To give thee from our royal master thanks,

speaker: ANGUS
text: Only to herald thee into his sight,

speaker: ANGUS
text: Not pay thee.

speaker: ROSS
text: And for an earnest of a greater honor,

speaker: ROSS
text: He bade me, from him, call thee Thane of Cawdor,

speaker: ROSS
text: In which addition, hail, most worthy thane,

speaker: ROSS
text: For it is thine.

speaker: BANQUO
text: What, can the devil speak true?

speaker: MACBETH
text: The Thane of Cawdor lives. Why do you dress me

speaker: MACBETH
text: In borrowed robes?

speaker: ANGUS
text: Who was the Thane lives yet,

speaker: ANGUS
text: But under heavy judgment bears that life

speaker: ANGUS
text: Which he deserves to lose. Whether he was combined

speaker: ANGUS
text: With those of Norway, or did line the rebel

speaker: ANGUS
text: With hidden help and vantage, or that with both

speaker: ANGUS
text: He labored in his country’s wrack, I know not;

speaker: ANGUS
text: But treasons capital, confessed and proved,

speaker: ANGUS
text: Have overthrown him.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Glamis and Thane of Cawdor!

speaker: MACBETH
text:
The greatest is behind.
                 
                To
                 
                Ross
                 
                and
                 
                Angus
                .
               Thanks for your pains.

speaker: MACBETH
text:
Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Banquo
                .
                 
              Do you not hope your children shall be kings,

speaker: MACBETH
text: When those that gave the Thane of Cawdor to me

speaker: MACBETH
text: Promised no less to them?

speaker: BANQUO
text: That, trusted home,

speaker: BANQUO
text: Might yet enkindle you unto the crown,

speaker: BANQUO
text: Besides the Thane of Cawdor. But ’tis strange.

speaker: BANQUO
text: And oftentimes, to win us to our harm,

speaker: BANQUO
text: The instruments of darkness tell us truths,

speaker: BANQUO
text: Win us with honest trifles, to betray ’s

speaker: BANQUO
text: In deepest consequence.—

speaker: BANQUO
text: Cousins, a word, I pray you.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Two truths are told

speaker: MACBETH
text: As happy prologues to the swelling act

speaker: MACBETH
text: Of the imperial theme.—I thank you, gentlemen.

speaker: MACBETH
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              This supernatural soliciting

speaker: MACBETH
text: Cannot be ill, cannot be good. If ill,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Why hath it given me earnest of success

speaker: MACBETH
text: Commencing in a truth? I am Thane of Cawdor.

speaker: MACBETH
text: If good, why do I yield to that suggestion

speaker: MACBETH
text: Whose horrid image doth unfix my hair

speaker: MACBETH
text: And make my seated heart knock at my ribs

speaker: MACBETH
text: Against the use of nature? Present fears

speaker: MACBETH
text: Are less than horrible imaginings.

speaker: MACBETH
text: My thought, whose murder yet is but fantastical,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Shakes so my single state of man

speaker: MACBETH
text: That function is smothered in surmise,

speaker: MACBETH
text: And nothing is but what is not.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Look how our partner’s rapt.

speaker: MACBETH
text: If chance will have me king, why, chance may crown me

speaker: MACBETH
text: Without my stir.

speaker: BANQUO
text: New honors come upon him,

speaker: BANQUO
text: Like our strange garments, cleave not to their mold

speaker: BANQUO
text: But with the aid of use.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Come what come may,

speaker: MACBETH
text: Time and the hour runs through the roughest day.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Worthy Macbeth, we stay upon your leisure.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Give me your favor. My dull brain was wrought

speaker: MACBETH
text: With things forgotten. Kind gentlemen, your pains

speaker: MACBETH
text: Are registered where every day I turn

speaker: MACBETH
text: The leaf to read them. Let us toward the King.

speaker: MACBETH
text:
Aside
                 
                to
                 
                Banquo
                .
                 
              Think upon what hath chanced,and at more time,

speaker: MACBETH
text: The interim having weighed it, let us speak

speaker: MACBETH
text: Our free hearts each to other.

speaker: BANQUO
text: Very gladly.

speaker: MACBETH
text: Till then, enough.—Come, friends.
